subject: Using agents in Cursor
body:
Explain unfamiliar code, refactor codebases, and iterate quickly  

Hi there,

Agents are more capable than ever. We’re seeing new default patterns emerge among developers using Cursor today.

Understand unfamiliar code

* “Explain how this feature works”
* “Why is this file structured this way?”

Why is this file structured this way?

Try this sample prompt ( https://cursor.com/link/prompt?text=why%20is%20this%20file%20structured%20this%20way%3F )

Implement scoped changes

* “Refactor this file to be simpler”
*
“Add tests for this code”

Add tests for this code

Try this sample prompt ( https://cursor.com/link/prompt?text=add%20tests%20for%20this%20code )

Make changes across files

* “Update the frontend to match this backend change”
*
“Add a new API endpoint”

Add a new API endpoint

Try this sample prompt ( https://cursor.com/link/prompt?text=add%20a%20new%20API%20endpoint )

Join an upcoming workshop ( https://cursor.com/workshops ) to learn about advanced Cursor workflows.

Best,
Cursor Team

The best way to code with AI.

To opt out of future emails, click unsubscribe ( https://track.customer.io/unsubscribe/dgT_pwsBANaj7x_Vo-8fAZzTWubSGPXQqgZXbdvl0Q== ).

Download Cursor ( https://cursor.com/download )    Use Cursor in browser ( https://cursor.com/agents )    Read the docs ( https://cursor.com/docs )    Join a workshop ( https://cursor.com/workshops )
